task_completion.description: Verify the location is set to the ALMA observatory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window is visible, showing 'ALMA' as the location, or the coordinates Latitude ~23.019° S, Longitude ~67.753° W, and Altitude 5050m.

task_completion.description: Verify the simulation date and time are set to July 15, 2024, at 03:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Date and Time window or the bottom toolbar displays the date '2024-07-15' and time '03:00'.

task_completion.description: Verify the display settings for the radio astronomy survey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Stellarium sky view, the landscape (ground) and atmosphere (sky glow) must be disabled. The Galactic Grid (intersecting coordinate lines) and Galactic Equator line must be enabled and visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the view is centered on the Sagittarius constellation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sagittarius constellation is visible and centered in the main Stellarium view, typically indicated by its label, stick figure, or selection marker.

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot was saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows a screenshot image file located in the `~/Pictures/stellarium/` directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the observation note was created with the required content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file `~/Desktop/galactic_survey_plan.txt` containing the exact words 'Sagittarius', 'ALMA', and 'July 15'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Stellarium application open with its configuration windows (Location, Date/Time, View settings) being used, rather than just terminal commands editing config files.

integrity.description: The observation note is a genuine text file created by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file is shown in a text editor or terminal, not just a hallucinated output or a copy-paste of the task description into a prompt.

integrity.description: The screenshot saved is an actual image file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file manager shows an image file icon or thumbnail in the `~/Pictures/stellarium/` directory, rather than a text file or script.
